title: Jackson Mann site could include housing, study finds ahead of community meeting
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu announced a community meeting on May 28 to discuss the future of Jackson Mann Community Center.
A city-commissioned study suggests housing could be included in the Jackson Mann site redevelopment in Allston.
The redevelopment plan faces constraints, with $10 million allocated for design in the city's current capital budget.

title: Here’s how hot meteorologists say it could get in Boston this week
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Boston could see record-breaking temperatures with forecasts predicting highs of 95F on Tuesday.
A major heat risk is forecast for cities from the Mid-Atlantic to New England, including Boston.
Potential for strong to severe thunderstorms with a cold front moving through Wednesday afternoon.

title: Here’s what Patriots could reportedly net in a Kayshon Boutte trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots' Kayshon Boutte may be traded with A.J. Brown's potential arrival, creating depth issues at wide receiver.
Boutte, a primary receiver last season, had 33 catches for 551 yards and six touchdowns in 14 games.
Patriots could trade Boutte for a fifth- or sixth-round draft pick, per Sports Illustrated's Albert Breer.

title: Fundraisers launched as 8 remain hospitalized days after fire, explosion at Maine lumber mill
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A fire and explosion at Robbins Lumber in Searsmont, Maine, on May 15 resulted in one firefighter's death and 12 injuries.
Investigation by Maine's State Fire Marshal and ATF is ongoing despite challenges from strong winds and debris at the site.
A GoFundMe campaign has raised over $30,000 for affected first responders, with community support for medical recovery needs.

title: Shams Charania expects Celtics’ pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo to carry into ‘this offseason’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Celtics show interest in acquiring Giannis Antetokounmpo, who is available for trade by the Milwaukee Bucks.
Shams Charania reports multiple teams, including the Celtics, Timberwolves, and Heat, are pursuing Antetokounmpo this offseason.
Antetokounmpo averaged 30.4 points, 11.9 rebounds, and 6.5 assists during his last full season with the Bucks.

title: T police: Man’s throat slashed in bus station altercation
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man's throat was slashed during an altercation at MBTA Nubian Square bus station in Roxbury.
The incident occurred around 8:06 p.m. following a verbal disagreement between two men, police reported.
The injured man received immediate aid and was taken to the hospital with non-life threatening injuries.

title: Red Sox get discouraging update on Roman Anthony’s injury after he tries swinging bat
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony of the Red Sox still faces soreness from a sprained ligament in his right finger.
Anthony's return is delayed; shut down from some activities until after rest period this Thursday.
Anthony played only 30 games this season, hindered by finger injury and previous health issues.

title: Bill Belichick gets candid on Tom Brady’s growth with Patriots: ‘He wasn’t even really good, but he became great’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick discussed Tom Brady's growth from a fourth-string quarterback to an NFL superstar on the 'Pardon My Take' podcast.
Belichick described Brady's early career as a game manager, gradually evolving into the focal point of opposing defenses by 2004.
Belichick, now coaching at UNC, shared his experiences coaching younger players and criticized the taste of coffee despite a prior Dunkin' ad.

title: As Minogue pours millions into his own campaign, state lawmaker pushes to ban self-funding
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gubernatorial candidate Mike Minogue self-funds campaign with $13.5 million, raising concerns over self-funding practices.
State Sen. Rebecca Rausch proposes amendment to ban unlimited self-funding in Massachusetts political campaigns.
Rausch's amendment will be considered in the Senate budget discussions this week, focusing on campaign finance reform.

title: Celtics fans voted NBA’s ‘most obnoxious’ in anonymous player poll
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics fans voted most passionate and most obnoxious in The Athletic's anonymous poll of 161 NBA players.
TD Garden named arena with most passionate fans, narrowly beating Madison Square Garden with 20.3% of votes.
Celtics not among top eight destinations for Giannis Antetokounmpo, despite reported interest in the Bucks star.
